summary: Matched and named the fighter special-attributes loader table and per-fighter load/copy-special-attributes routines across all 33 fighters. The PR replaced many anonymous copy routines and table labels with ft_LoadSpecialAttrs and ft<Char>_LoadSpecialAttrs names, introduced COPY_ATTRS and SCALE_HEIGHT_ATTRS helpers in fighter.h, moved several small ASM-only copy routines into C, and resolved GameWatch merge-conflict fallout. It also normalized airborne special StartAction names from SpecialNAir/SpecialSAir/SpecialHiAir/SpecialLwAir to SpecialAirN/SpecialAirS/SpecialAirHi/SpecialAirLw.
what_changed: Renamed the global function-pointer table previously labeled lbl_803C1EBC to ft_LoadSpecialAttrs and updated references in asm/melee/ft/code_800877F8.s to index that named table.

Populated ft_LoadSpecialAttrs with per-fighter entries such as ftMario_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftFox_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftCFalcon_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftDonkey_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftKirby_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftGameWatch_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftMasterHand_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftCrazyHand_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftGKoopa_LoadSpecialAttrs, and ftSandbag_LoadSpecialAttrs.
Converted many small special-attribute copy routines from anonymous names into C-level ft<Char>_LoadSpecialAttrs routines that copy x10C_ftData->ext_attr into x2D4_specialAttributes.
Added COPY_ATTRS(gobj, attributeName) in fighter.h to standardize the repeated typed copy pattern from fighter data ext_attr to runtime special attributes.
Added SCALE_HEIGHT_ATTRS(num_attrs) in fighter.h for fighters whose special attributes contain a height_attributes array that must be multiplied by the fighter's y scale.
Preserved character-specific post-copy scaling behavior for characters such as Pikachu, Samus, Link, and Mewtwo rather than replacing those routines with a blind copy only.

Renamed several clone/dependent loader wrappers to call base-character loaders, for example ftDrMario_LoadSpecialAttrs calls ftMario_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftFalco_LoadSpecialAttrs calls ftFox_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftGanon_LoadSpecialAttrs calls ftCFalcon_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftRoy_LoadSpecialAttrs calls ftMars_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftCLink_LoadSpecialAttrs calls ftLink_LoadSpecialAttrs, and ftGKoopa_LoadSpecialAttrs calls ftKoopa_LoadSpecialAttrs.
Removed or superseded several matching ASM copy routines in character assembly files as their C equivalents landed.

Renamed airborne special action-entry symbols to the project convention SpecialAirN/SpecialAirS/SpecialAirHi/SpecialAirLw, replacing forms such as SpecialNAir_StartAction and SpecialHiAir_StartAction.
Adjusted relevant headers and prototypes in include/functions.h, include/variables.h, and character headers to reflect the new LoadSpecialAttrs names.
Fixed merge conflicts with GameWatch per the PR body; exact conflict details are not described beyond the changed GameWatch assembly/C files and title/body evidence.
smart_moves: Recognized that many anonymous routines shared the same semantic pattern: restore/copy special attributes from ftData ext_attr into Fighter.x2D4_specialAttributes.
Centralized the common copy idiom in COPY_ATTRS, reducing future handwritten pointer boilerplate and making the intended data flow explicit.
Kept scaling logic outside the generic macro where needed, which avoided losing fighter-specific behavior for non-default model scale cases.
Named the table ft_LoadSpecialAttrs instead of leaving it as a raw data label, making the dispatch in code_800877F8.s understandable and searchable.
Used thin wrapper functions for clone characters whose special attributes reuse base-character logic, preserving table shape while avoiding duplicated implementations.
Applied a broad naming cleanup to airborne special StartAction symbols in the same sweep, aligning callsites and assembly labels with a consistent Air-before-direction convention.
decomp_lessons: When a table indexes one function per fighter in roster order, naming the table and every slot can unlock a large batch of related functions even if each implementation is small.
Repeated memcpy-like handwritten assembly over x10C_ftData->ext_attr and x2D4_specialAttributes is strong evidence for a LoadSpecialAttrs/reset-special-attrs routine.
Generic macros are useful for matching small attribute-copy functions, but character-specific scale adjustments must be audited before replacing routines wholesale.
Clone fighters often belong in the same dispatch table with wrapper functions that delegate to the base fighter's loader; this preserves the ABI/table layout while expressing code reuse.
Do not infer that every LoadSpecialAttrs function is just a struct assignment: examples in this PR still scale selected attributes after COPY_ATTRS.
Large table renames should be accompanied by prototype/header updates so future C files can reference named functions instead of anonymous func_ addresses.
Symbol convention matters for searchability: SpecialAirN/SpecialAirS/SpecialAirHi/SpecialAirLw is more consistent than mixing direction before Air.
naming_conventions: Per-character loader functions: ft<Char>_LoadSpecialAttrs, for example ftCFalcon_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftFox_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftPikachu_LoadSpecialAttrs, ftGameWatch_LoadSpecialAttrs.
Clone wrappers retain clone names but call base loaders, for example ftDrMario_LoadSpecialAttrs -> ftMario_LoadSpecialAttrs and ftFalco_LoadSpecialAttrs -> ftFox_LoadSpecialAttrs.

Airborne special action names use SpecialAir<Direction>_StartAction, for example ftFox_SpecialAirN_StartAction, ftDonkey_SpecialAirS_StartAction, ftCFalcon_SpecialAirLw_StartAction, ftMars_SpecialAirHi_StartAction.
Attribute struct names were made more character-specific in at least one case, such as CaptainAttr becoming ftCFalconAttributes.
Pikachu/Samus-style scaled attribute arrays were named height_attributes rather than raw xE0_array_start-style fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched repeated PowerPC loops that loaded Fighter from gobj->0x2C, read ftData at x10C, copied ftData->ext_attr into x2D4_specialAttributes, and used mtctr/bdnz copy loops.
Kept some implementations in assembly where not yet decompiled but renamed labels to meaningful LoadSpecialAttrs symbols, as seen with ftKirby_LoadSpecialAttrs.
Used direct struct assignment through typed pointers in C to reproduce the copy-loop behavior generated by the compiler.
Introduced macro expansion for the repeated local names ft, sA2, and ext_attr, which likely helped maintain matching codegen across many tiny routines.
Updated the table base references in assembly from lbl_803C1EBC@ha/@l to ft_LoadSpecialAttrs@ha/@l without changing the underlying address/addi sequence.
Used wrapper functions for clones to match a call to the base loader rather than duplicating a copy sequence.
Preserved post-copy scale multiplications after COPY_ATTRS for scale-sensitive attributes, including height attribute arrays via SCALE_HEIGHT_ATTRS.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the slice.
The PR received an APPROVED review from RevoSucks shortly before merge.
The PR body requested accepting PR #408 before this one, or accepting this one and closing PR #408, indicating overlap with an earlier load_special_attrs effort.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refine COPY_ATTRS or remove it in favor of an inline/static helper.
Search for remaining func_ LoadSpecialAttrs-like routines that copy x10C_ftData->ext_attr to x2D4_specialAttributes.
Audit all ft_LoadSpecialAttrs table entries against fighter kind order to ensure clone and boss slots stay aligned.
Search for remaining SpecialNAir_StartAction or SpecialHiAir_StartAction names that should follow SpecialAir<Direction> convention.
Compare PR #408 to this PR to understand which load_special_attrs work was superseded.
